2026-04-23T02:57:27.5776746Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5777846Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5778542Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5779064Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5779396Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5779653Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5779824Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5780009Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5780191Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5780436Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-23T02:57:27.5780980Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T02:57:27.5781269Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-23T03:00:33.3455294Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T02:58:29.400001709Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58541,\"title\":\"[DataBox] Docs: README has stale copy-paste content and missing service-specific examples\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit 96635cf97 (#58496) by `@azure-sdk`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nCommit #58496 added the `Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox` management library to the repository, including generated resource types (`DataBoxJobResource`, `DataBoxJobCollection`), custom model extensions, and a README/CHANGELOG.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Wrong service description:** The README states _\\u0026#34;This library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources.\\u0026#34;_ — this is stale copy-paste from a different package and is factually incorrect for DataBox.\\n- **No DataBox-specific Key Concepts:** The \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section only contains a generic link; it does not describe DataBox-specific concepts (job types, data copy configurations, job stages, shipping/transfer workflows).\\n- **No inline code examples:** The \\u0026#34;Examples\\u0026#34; section only links to a generic samples page with no DataBox-specific code showing how to create, list, or manage DataBox jobs.\\n- **No test-backed README snippets:** There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-annotated test methods; only auto-generated samples exist under `tests/Generated/Samples/`. The README therefore has no `Snippet:` placeholders for `Update-Snippets.ps1` to populate.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### Fix the wrong service description in `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md`\\n\\nEdit the file. Replace:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThis library supports managing Microsoft Azure Customer Insights resources.\\n```\\n\\nWith:\\n\\n```markdown\\nThis library supports managing Microsoft Azure Data Box resources, enabling you to create and monitor Data Box jobs that transfer large amounts of data to and from Azure.\\n```\\n\\n#### Add DataBox-specific Key Concepts in `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md`\\n\\nReplace the existing \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; section body with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\nThe following are the main resource types exposed by this library:\\n\\n- **DataBoxJob** – Represents a Data Box order. A job tracks the full lifecycle of a data-transfer request: validation, device shipment, data copy, and upload to Azure storage.\\n- **Job stages** – A `DataBoxJobStage` describes the current phase of a job (e.g., `DeviceOrdered`, `DeviceDelivered`, `DataCopied`, `Completed`).\\n- **DataBoxBasicJobDetails** – Contains configuration details for a job, including the data copy settings, contact information, and shipping address.\\n- **Shipping address** – A `DataBoxShippingAddress` describes where the physical device should be shipped.\\n\\nKey concepts of the Azure Resource Manager client library for .NET can be found [here]((azure.github.io/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n#### 2a. Create a sample test file\\n\\nCreate the file `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/tests/Samples/Sample1_DataBoxJob.cs` with content:\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Models;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_DataBoxJob : SamplesBase\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task CreateDataBoxJob()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:CreateDataBoxJob\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string jobName = \\u0026#34;myDataBoxJob\\u0026#34;;\\n            AzureLocation location = AzureLocation.WestUS;\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            DataBoxJobCollection jobs = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxJobs();\\n\\n            // Build job details (DataBox device, storage account destination)\\n            var jobDetails = new DataBoxJobDetails(\\n                new DataBoxContactDetails(\\n                    contactName: \\u0026#34;Test User\\u0026#34;,\\n                    phone: \\u0026#34;1234567890\\u0026#34;,\\n                    emailList: new[] { \\u0026#34;test@example.com\\u0026#34; }),\\n                new DataBoxShippingAddress(\\n                    streetAddress1: \\u0026#34;1 Microsoft Way\\u0026#34;,\\n                    city: \\u0026#34;Redmond\\u0026#34;,\\n                    stateOrProvince: \\u0026#34;WA\\u0026#34;,\\n                    country: \\u0026#34;US\\u0026#34;,\\n                    postalCode: \\u0026#34;98052\\u0026#34;),\\n                new DataBoxDataDestinationDetails(\\n                    storageAccountId: new ResourceIdentifier(\\n                        $\\u0026#34;/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts/mystorageaccount\\u0026#34;)));\\n\\n            var jobData = new DataBoxJobData(location, jobDetails);\\n            ArmOperation operation = await jobs.CreateOrUpdateAsync(\\n                WaitUntil.Completed, jobName, jobData);\\n            DataBoxJobResource job = operation.Value;\\n\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Created job: {job.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [SyncOnly]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListDataBoxJobs()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ListDataBoxJobs\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n\\n            string subscriptionId = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n            string resourceGroupName = \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;;\\n\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = client.GetSubscriptionResource(\\n                SubscriptionResource.CreateResourceIdentifier(subscriptionId));\\n            ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription\\n                .GetResourceGroupAsync(resourceGroupName);\\n\\n            DataBoxJobCollection jobs = resourceGroup.GetDataBoxJobs();\\n\\n            await foreach (DataBoxJobResource job in jobs.GetAllAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Job: {job.Data.Name} — Status: {job.Data.Status}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### 2b. Add snippet placeholders to `sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/README.md`\\n\\nIn the **Examples** section, replace the existing content with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Examples\\n\\n### Create a DataBox job\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List DataBox jobs in a resource group\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\nFor more code samples, see [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted)\\n```\\n\\n(Note: each fenced block above should use triple backticks as normal markdown fences in the actual README file.)\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README currently has most required sections but is missing or thin in:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Install | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Prerequisites | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Getting started \\u0026gt; Authenticate | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic only | Replace with DataBox-specific content (Step 1 above) |\\n| Examples | ⚠️ Link-only, no code | Add snippet placeholders (Step 2b above) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Present | No change |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Present | No change |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 databox` — injects snippet code from `#region Snippet:` tags into README placeholders; verify with `git diff`\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 databox` — regenerates public API listing files (no new public types expected, but run to be safe)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/databox/Azure.ResourceManager.DataBox/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
